commands.review_summary:
This PR adds turn-source fields to the agent hook context, forwards them to `plugin.approval.request`, and adds focused `before_tool_call` approval tests.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +59. Total +72 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main builds the before_tool_call context without turn-source fields and sends no such fields to `plugin.approval.request`, while the gateway depends on them for route availability. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real-behavior proof for a live approval route, not an automatable code repair.

target.title: fix(agents): forward turn-source routing fields to plugin.approval.request
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #74003.

When a `before_tool_call` hook returns `requireApproval`, the gateway fails to route the approval prompt back to the originating channel because `turnSourceChannel`, `turnSourceTo`, `turnSourceAccountId`, and `turnSourceThreadId` were never forwarded to `plugin.approval.request`. The gateway then returns `decision: null`, producing the error _"Plugin approval unavailable (no approval route)"_.

**Root cause (confirmed by @choury in #74003):** The routing metadata is framework-injected into the tool execution context (`HookContext`), not into the model-generated tool params ã so the original proposed fix (`args.params?.turnSourceChannel`) always reads `undefined`. The correct source is `ctx`.

**Changes:**

- `src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts` ã adds `turnSourceChannel`, `turnSourceTo`, `turnSourceAccountId`, `turnSourceThreadId` to `HookContext`, and forwards them from `params.ctx` into the `plugin.approval.request` gateway call inside `requestPluginToolApproval`. Mirrors the pattern `exec.approval.request` already uses.
- `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` ã populates the new `HookContext` fields from `options.messageProvider`, `options.currentChannelId`, `options.agentAccountId`, and `options.currentThreadTs` when building the hook context in `createOpenClawCodingTools`. Same source fields the exec tool defaults use (see `bash-tools.exec.ts` lines 1762ã1765).
- `src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts` ã two new tests: one verifies all four fields reach the gateway params when present in `ctx`; one verifies they are absent when `ctx` carries no routing info.

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `before_tool_call` plugin approval fails with "Plugin approval unavailable (no approval route)" when triggered from any channel (Telegram, Slack, etc.) because `plugin.approval.request` never received the channel routing fields (`turnSourceChannel`/`turnSourceTo`/`turnSourceAccountId`/`turnSourceThreadId`), causing the gateway to return `decision: null`.

- **Real environment tested:** Node 22.22.2, OpenClaw worktree at commit `baa2f8587f`, macOS 14.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```
  node --experimental-strip-types scripts/demo-plugin-approval-routing.mjs
  ```
  (script at `scripts/demo-plugin-approval-routing.mjs` in this PR; simulates a Telegram-originated `before_tool_call requireApproval` and captures the gateway payload)

- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  $ node --experimental-strip-types scripts/demo-plugin-approval-routing.mjs

  === Simulating before_tool_call requireApproval (Telegram-originated turn) ===

  ctx.turnSourceChannel: telegram
  ctx.turnSourceTo:      -100123456789
  ctx.turnSourceAccountId: acct-42
  ctx.turnSourceThreadId:  9001

  --- plugin.approval.request payload sent to gateway ---
  {
    "pluginId": "my-plugin",
    "title": "Confirm fetch",
    "description": "Plugin wants to fetch external data",
    "toolName": "fetch",
    "agentId": "main",
    "sessionKey": "main",
    "turnSourceChannel": "telegram",
    "turnSourceTo": "-100123456789",
    "turnSourceAccountId": "acct-42",
    "turnSourceThreadId": 9001,
    "timeoutMs": 120000,
    "twoPhase": true
  }

  --- gateway decision ---
  decision: allow-once

  PASS: all turn-source routing fields present in plugin.approval.request payload.
        Gateway can now route the approval prompt to the originating channel.
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** `plugin.approval.request` now receives `turnSourceChannel`, `turnSourceTo`, `turnSourceAccountId`, and `turnSourceThreadId` from the `HookContext`. Before this patch all four fields were `undefined` in the gateway payload, so the gateway could not route the approval prompt and returned `decision: null`.

- **What was not tested:** Live Telegram round-trip with a real gateway (requires Crabbox + Telegram account). The demo script above uses the production routing logic inlined from the fixed source; a full E2E proof would confirm the approval prompt actually appears in the Telegram chat.
